title: Strill
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Strill-tur.jpg]] |name=Strill |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Semi-sentient]]''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'' |class=[[Mammal/Legends|Mammal]]''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' |subspecies= |races= |height= |length=1 [[Meter/Legends|meter]] |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor= |haircolor=[[Color/Legends|Gold]]''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'' or gray''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' |feathers= |eyecolor=Gold |distinctions=*Sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] *Six [[Limb/Legends|legs]] *Hermaphroditic [[Reproduction/Legends|reproduction]] *Loose folds of skin *Repulsive odor |lifespan=300–400 [[Standard year/Legends|years]]The novel ''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'' states that strills can live up to three to four times the average human life span, which the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]] defines as one hundred years. |origin=[[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'' |habitat=Mandalore's northern [[Forest/Legends|forests]] |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|What's a strill?''"
"''A disgustingly ugly, smelly Mandalorian hunting animal. You can't mistake it for any other species, not even in ''this'' menagerie of a city.|[[Perrive]] and [[Kal Skirata]]|''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]''}} '''Strills''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|highly intelligent]], [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]] [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. The one-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long, six-[[Limb/Legends|legged]] creatures, with their loose folds of [[Leather/Legends|leathery]] skin, were generally considered physically unattractive by many beings, and their uniquely pungent odor was off-putting to most [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]]. However, a number of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] took to keeping strills—or '''''striile''''' in [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']]—as pets, making use of their naturally ferocious predatory instincts as [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] animals, and the long-lived creatures often became life-long companions of their Mandalorian masters. One such strill by the name of [[Lord Mirdalan]] was the constant partner of the Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] [[Walon Vau]], and the two were nearly inseparable until the [[Time/Legends|time]] of Vau's [[Death/Legends|death]], whereupon the affectionately nicknamed "Mird" was entrusted to close friend [[Jaing Skirata]] for care. Both doted upon the creature, and in return, enjoyed fierce loyalty from the strill. Strills also behaved in a maternal fashion in the presence of a [[Reproduction/Legends|pregnant]] female or [[Youngling/Legends|infant]], a trait that manifested as an innate protectiveness toward the female or youngling and an instinct to construct a nest. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|Can Mird have pups?''"
"''Mird can bear pups ''and'' sire them. But don't ask me how hermaphroditic reproduction works in practice. All I know is that if Mird meets the strill of its dreams, then they end up with a litter of little strills.|[[Nyreen Vollen]] and [[Walon Vau]], discussing the strill, [[Lord Mirdalan]]|}} Strills were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of predatory animals [[Homeworld/Legends|indigenous]] to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], measuring close to a [[Meter/Legends|meter]] in length, on average. They were [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]] that utilized hermaphroditic [[Reproduction/Legends|reproduction]]; strills were equally capable of both siring and bearing offspring with a second strill. Young strills were commonly referred to as pups. Covered in thick fur of a gold or gray-tan [[Color/Legends|color]], strills possessed numerous folds of loose, [[Leather/Legends|leathery]] skin that stretched all the way to its thin, [[whip]]-like tail. These skin folds could be used to form wing membranes between their six strong [[Limb/Legends|legs]], that allowed the creatures to glide from high places and even fly short distances by flapping their legs in unison. Furthermore, strills were capable of carrying substantial loads while flying, including the weight of a full-grown [[Human/Legends|Human]] male. Strills could live up to three or four times the span of a natural human's lifetime, and it was common for [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] owners to entrust their beloved pets to their clan kinsmen following their [[Death/Legends|deaths]]. Strills had gold-colored [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], gray [[Tongue/Legends|tongues]], and sharp claws. Their wide [[Mouth/Legends|mouths]] were filled with sharp [[Tooth/Legends|fangs]], perfectly adapted for a [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]] diet and powerful enough to bite through [[Skeleton/Legends|skull]] [[Bone/Legends|bone]]; they were also known for the abundant amount of saliva which they excreted. Strills could withstand significantly cold temperatures for extended periods of time, and their [[Ear/Legends|hearing]] was far more acute than a Human's. Aside from these features, one of the most identifiable traits of a strill was the strong, acrid stench they gave off. To male Humans, the smell was often considered repulsive, but an individual could become acclimated to the scent with continued exposure. Human females and non-[[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] species, though, did not find the strill's scent displeasing, contributing to its continued effectiveness as a [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]]. Unfortunately, strills were susceptible to [[Brain/Legends|brain]] [[Tumor/Legends|tumors]] which could, as a result of their presence, drive the animals to irrational acts of anger and aggression, even to the point of attacking their owner.''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' ==Behavior and intelligence== {{Quote|Is a strill safe around a small child?''"
"''Being hermaphroditic, all strills have a maternal streak, Ordo. Hence the endless nest building when it sees the baby.|[[Ordo Skirata]] and [[Walon Vau]]|}} [[File:LordMirdalan-SWG5.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Lord Mirdalan]], a strill owned by Walon Vau]] Strills were highly intelligent creatures, capable of understanding verbal commands and intuiting the feelings of those [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] around it. They made a variety of noises, including baying while hunting, whines and moans when under stress, and squeaks and low grumbles when pleased. Strills were territorial creatures, marking the areas they claimed as their own by rubbing their saliva-covered jowls and spraying from their strong scent glands around a chosen perimeter. Domesticated strills rarely left their owners, but occasionally did roam within the boundaries of their predefined territory independently. The capability of bearing young that all strill possessed also gave them a strong maternal instinct, and they could often sense when a nearby female was pregnant. Despite their predatory ferocity, strills were extremely protective of their young and the offspring of those within its social sphere. Human [[Youngling/Legends|infants]] had no need to fear a nearby strill and, in fact, strills were known to construct nests for human children just as they would for their own pups, using [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]], [[Blanket/Legends|blankets]], or any other soft materials that were readily available. Strills were skilled trackers and fierce hunters, capable of taking down prey larger than themselves, such as [[shatual]]s, with whom they shared their [[Forest/Legends|forest]] habitat. Strills could carry the carcass of their downed prey a significant distance back to their abode. The set of sharp claws located on each paw allowed a strill to climb the sides of buildings and [[Tree/Legends|trees]], where they would wait to ambush a potential target from overhead. Once the prospective prey was within range, the strill leaped from their high perch and, using their leathery wings formed from their loose folds of skin, glided through the air, swooping down for the kill. Strills were known to enjoy [[Milk/Legends|milk]] as well as meat, and preferred to eat their own kill after a hunt, though they were not opposed to sharing the spoils with its owner and associates. Strills also hunted to acquire food for its young or any offspring it felt solicitous toward. When domesticated, strills were extremely loyal and protective of their masters and their surrounding families. ==History== {{Quote|I promised Mird's last owner I'd look after it when he passed to the ''manda''. Strills live a lot longer than we do.''"
"''Heard of them, but never seen one.''"
"''They're nearly extinct on Mandalore. Mird—well, you might say it's a black ops strill. Saw a lot of commando action in a few wars.|[[Jaing Skirata]] and [[Mirta Gev]]|''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''}} Indigenous to the planet Mandalore, strills adapted to living and hunting in the Outer Rim world's [[veshok]] forests. During the course of Mandalore's long history, the Mandalorian people took to capturing strills, domesticating them, and keeping the creatures as pets. Their intelligence, hunting skills, and incredible loyalty were praised by their Mandalorian masters, and it was not uncommon for the long-lived animals to be passed down through a clan's generations. In the Mandalorian [[Language/Legends|language]] of [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']], the word for strill was ''striil''.{{WebCite|url=http://www.karentraviss.com/html/mando.htm|text=Ke jorhaa'ir Mando'a!|author=Karen Traviss|archivedate=20090923140534}} [[Mandalore the Avenger]] once owned a Strill. One strill by the name of [[Lord Mirdalan]]—often known by the affectionate nickname of Mird, or ''Mird'ika''—was the friend and pet of the Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] [[Walon Vau]]. Mird had been with Vau for the majority of his life leading up to the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and was his near constant companion. The strill followed Vau to the [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] world of [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] when he was chosen by [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] to be a member of the ''[[Cuy'val Dar]]'', a group of training [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeants]] tasked with training the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic's]] [[Special forces/Legends|special forces]] [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]], and it was on Kamino that Mird developed an enjoyment for stalking [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoans]] within [[Tipoca City/Legends|Tipoca City]]. On one occasion, the strill even killed a Kaminoan who was a member of the blue-eyed lower caste. When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Mird joined Vau and a number of [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] in helping to [[Mission to Coruscant (Clone Wars)|bring down]] a [[Jabiimi]] [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] cell responsible for a number of [[Bomb/Legends|bombing]] attacks on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. After Vau [[Verpine shatter gun|shot]] and killed the [[Perrive|terrorist leader]], Mird was responsible for recovering the body and the criminal files held with it. In the second year of the Clone Wars, Vau raided the [[Dressian Kiolsh Merchant Bank]] on the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]] world of [[Mygeeto/Legends|Mygeeto]], in order to steal the inheritance his estranged [[Walon Vau's mother|mother]] and [[Walon Vau's father|father]] had denied him when he left [[Irmenu|home]] to join the Mandalorians. During the course of his escape from the [[Bank/Legends|bank]], Vau fell through the Mygeetan ice; Mird refused to abandon its master and attempted to keep Vau warm with its own body until rescue arrived in the form of fellow ''Cuy'val Dar'' member [[Kal Skirata]], who extracted the strill and its master to safety. [[Etain Tur-Mukan]], whom Skirata would later [[Gai bal manda|adopt]], had her son [[Venku Skirata|Venku]] later that year, and Mird subsequently became very protective of the child. It also built nests for the baby on a number of occasions, collecting sheets and clothing from its human companions in order to do so. The strill later became a resident of Skirata's clan home on Mandalore, [[Kyrimorut/Legends|Kyrimorut]], marking the land around the [[Farm/Legends|farm]] and homestead as its own, and preferred to spend its time hunting or simply laying about, basking in the attention of its master and the other residents of Kyrimorut. As of the year [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], strills had become a rare sight on Mandalore, to the point which they were thought to be nearly extinct. By that time, Lord Mirdalan had entered into the possession of Kal Skirata's adopted son, [[Jaing Skirata]], entrusted to him upon the death of his former master, Vau. Mird continued to act as Skirata's partner in his exploits as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], and in [[41 ABY]], the two attended a feast in honor of the [[Wedding of Ghes Orade and Mirta Gev|wedding]] of [[Ghes Orade]] and [[Mirta Gev]] held in Mandalore's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]]. During this time, the ''[[Oyu'baat]]'' [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]] and [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcaf]] in Keldabe displayed a sign over the front door in which the words "No Strills Inside" was inscribed in both ''Mando'a'' and [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. However, [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] patron [[Baltan Carid]] jovially suggested on one occasion that a strill was, in fact, the resident of the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} executive suite, and would need to be kicked out before the room could be rented to anyone else. At some point in time, another strill had been kept by a friend of the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]-turned-Mandalorian by the name of [[Bardan Jusik|Gotab]]. The strill was beloved by its owner, but the creature eventually developed a malignant brain tumor that drove it to irrational acts of aggression. After the strill attacked several people—including both Gotab and it's owner—the decision was made to euthanize the animal in order to end its suffering and bring an end to the strill's unintended violence. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|We ''[Mandalorians]'' keep strills. Hunting animals, the ones with folded skin and six legs that you might have seen around.|[[Bardan Jusik|Gotab]]|}} The strill was first introduced to the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe via a mention in the [[2004]] novel, ''[[Republic Commando: Hard Contact]]'', by [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Karen Traviss]].''[[Republic Commando: Hard Contact]]'' The initial vague reference to an animal adept at hunting and tracking would later be fully realized in the novel's sequel, [[Republic Commando: Triple Zero|''Triple Zero'']], where the strill known as Lord Mirdalan made its debut. Lord Mirdalan went on to become a recurring character in Traviss' [[Republic Commando|''Republic'' and ''Imperial Commando'' series]], featuring in the subsequent [[Republic Commando: True Colors|''True Colors'']], [[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel|''Order 66'']], and ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'' novels. To date, Lord Mirdalan has been the most prominently featured strill in the ''Star Wars'' universe. The species received its first official visual depiction in the form of a sketch of Lord Mirdalan, created by [[artist]] [[Shea Standefer]] for the trading card set ''[[2010 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 5]]''.{{Topps|set=2010 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 5|cardname=Lord Mirdalan collectible sketch card}} Another image of a strill was later included in the [[2010]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|''Star Wars'' RPG]] supplement book, ''[[The Unknown Regions]]'', published by [[Wizards of the Coast]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Legacy of the Sith]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Republic Commando: Hard Contact]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'' *''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'' *''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'' *''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' *''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{Topps|set=2010 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 5|cardname=Lord Mirdalan collectible sketch card}} {{1stp}} *''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Strill |de=Strill |pl=Strill }} [[Category:Carnivorous creatures]] [[Category:Creatures of Mandalore]] [[Category:Domesticated creatures]] [[Category:Flying creatures]] [[Category:Hermaphroditic creatures]] [[Category:Jungle and forest creatures]] [[Category:Mammals]] [[Category:Semi-sentient species]]
